_note: TKT-0091 / ADR-0143 — ui-timeline-item recursive nesting, shared accordion, collapsed-summary preview. Extends the ALREADY-SHIPPED timeline family (timeline-family-ship.decomp.json) rather than rebuilding it — nodes here touch ONLY timeline-item.ts/.css/.md plus the SPEC/LLD amendment already landed as docs-only. OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the anatomy/geometry/docs surfaces this extension touches. INSIDE-OUT actions = what the extended item must newly DO, crossed via hosts. plan:true — every leaf carries a checkable accept predicate; timeline.ts/disclosure.ts/status-stream.ts are UNTOUCHED (verified no-op for timeline.ts's terminal-connector marking, ADR-0143's own finding) so no nodes exist for them. Catalog wiring (ADR-0143 F6) is explicitly NOT a node here — a deferred, separate a2ui-builder intake.

nodes.label: ui-timeline-item — recursive nesting + shared accordion + collapsed-summary preview
the [data-role="nested"] adoption slot (F1) — a genuine nested <ui-timeline> child, adopted like [data-role="detail"] today
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — jsdom: a <ui-timeline-item> authored with a <ui-timeline data-role="nested"> light-DOM child adopts it (moved, not cloned — ADR-0022) exactly once at connect (idempotent guard); the descriptor gains a `nested` slot entry mirroring `detail`'s shape

nodes.label: the shared composed ui-disclosure (F2) — adopts detail THEN nested, one caret, materializes only if either exists
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — jsdom: an item with ONLY detail composes a disclosure with just that content (byte-identical to today's shipped behavior, a regression guard); an item with ONLY nested composes a disclosure with just the nested <ui-timeline>; an item with BOTH composes ONE disclosure containing detail's content followed by nested's, in that order; an item with NEITHER composes no disclosure at all (unchanged from today)

nodes.label: recursion depth — a nested <ui-timeline> item itself carries its own [data-role="nested"] child, arbitrary depth
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — jsdom: a 3-level-deep authored nesting (item > nested timeline > item > nested timeline > item) connects cleanly with no depth cap, no error, no infinite-loop guard needed (the recursion is authored markup depth, not runtime-generated)

nodes.label: the confirmed no-op — timeline.ts terminal-connector marking needs no change
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline` — timeline.ts and timeline.test.ts stay byte-UNMODIFIED by this build (`:scope > ui-timeline-item` already self-scopes per nesting level, verified by ADR-0143's own precedent read); a NEW regression case added to timeline-item.test.ts (the file this build DOES touch) asserts a nested ui-timeline's own last child gets `[data-last]`, independent of and un-interfered-with by the OUTER timeline's own last-item marking

nodes.label: collapsed-summary preview (F3) — last nested descendant in DOM order, painted into the EXISTING trailing cell, live-updating, collapsed-only
last-descendant resolution — recurse to the deepest last <ui-timeline-item> in DOM order (last child wins, no status-priority)
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — jsdom: a nested timeline with 3 items resolves the preview source to the LAST one; a 2-level-deep nesting (the last item of the outer nested timeline itself has ITS OWN further-nested timeline) resolves to the DEEPEST leaf item, not the intermediate one; an item with no nested slot never attempts resolution (a plain no-op, not an error)

nodes.label: the MutationObserver on the nested subtree — recomputes on childList/status/label/characterData changes
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — jsdom: appending a new last item to the nested timeline AFTER connect updates the resolved preview source; changing the current last item's `status` or `label` attribute updates it too; the observer is installed once per nested-slot presence and disconnected in disconnected() (the toast-region/disclosure heal-observer teardown precedent)

nodes.label: the trailing paint gate — write only while the disclosure is CLOSED and trailing is NOT consumer-owned; clear while open
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — jsdom: with the disclosure open, `trailing`'s cell is empty/unwritten (the real nested content is directly visible instead — no duplicate); closing the disclosure paints the resolved last-descendant's label + a status-shape mirror into trailing; a consumer-authored `[data-role="trailing"]` child (today's existing consumer-owned path, `#consumerOwned.has('trailing')`) is NEVER overwritten by this new effect at any open/closed state — the exact regression guard the wrapper-trap class of bug already tests elsewhere in this file

nodes.label: connector geometry (F4) — zero CSS changes; a nested rail is fully independent, visually indented by the disclosure body's own padding
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser timeline-item` — timeline-item.css is UNCHANGED by this build (a diff-based regression check, or: every existing geometry probe in timeline-item's browser suite still passes unmodified); a nested ui-timeline's items render their OWN complete marker+connector rail, visually offset from the parent's rail by the disclosure body's inline padding, cross-engine

nodes.label: ARIA (F5) — zero new machinery; nested list is a legal role=list>listitem>role=list structure; closed disclosure hides descendants natively
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser timeline-item` — an OPEN item with nested content exposes the nested list/listitems in the accessibility tree (a real AT-tree probe, not just DOM presence); a CLOSED item's nested descendants are absent from BOTH the accessibility tree and the tab order (the native <details> guarantee, asserted directly rather than assumed); the trailing preview text IS present in the accessible tree while closed (it is ordinary visible content, never aria-hidden)

nodes.label: timeline-item.md descriptor updated — the nested slot, the trailing cell's new auto-fill behavior noted, no new props/events
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` (timeline-item-descriptor.test.ts, the contract trip-wire) — the descriptor's `slots[]` gains the `nested` entry; NO new `attributes[]`/`properties[]`/`events[]` entries (the mechanism is pure anatomy + an internal effect, no new public surface — verified against ADR-0143's own F1-F6 decisions, none of which add a prop or event); compareDescriptorToProps stays green (nothing to drift, since static props is unchanged)

nodes.label: family-coherence + barrels + size — unchanged token surface, no new import edges beyond the existing timeline-item -> disclosure/timeline composition
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green (family-coherence.test.ts, barrels.test.ts, layering.test.ts) — timeline-item.ts's import of '../timeline/timeline.ts' (needed to reference UITimelineElement's type for the [data-role="nested"] check, if typed) does not create a cross-folder cycle (timeline already imports timeline-item, so this is timeline-item -> timeline -> timeline-item; MUST be resolved as a type-only import or a runtime-safe non-cyclic reference — a real risk this leaf's own test must prove, not assume); `npm run size` run by hand, marginal re-measured only if it moved materially

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer GO before commit
nodes.accept: a fresh-context component-reviewer pass returns GO for the extended ui-timeline-item against ADR-0143's fork sheet (anatomy, the shared-disclosure ordering, the collapsed-summary live-update + paint-gate correctness, connector/ARIA no-new-machinery claims) — generator ≠ critic

nodes.label: size does NOT cascade (F7) — a nested <ui-timeline> reads its own [size], unaffected by its ancestor's
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — jsdom: a parent ui-timeline-item with size="lg" hosting an unauthored nested <ui-timeline> (no size attribute) resolves the nested timeline's own items to the DEFAULT 'md' register, not 'lg' — a negative control proving no inheritance/forwarding mechanism exists; a nested <ui-timeline size="sm"> resolves its own items to 'sm' regardless of the parent's size, proving each level is independently authored

actions.label: adopt a nested <ui-timeline> child via a new [data-role="nested"] slot, reusing ui-timeline itself
compose ONE shared disclosure wrapping detail then nested, materializing only when either exists
support arbitrary authored recursion depth with no cap and no new mechanism per level
leave the outer host's terminal-connector marking untouched — it already self-scopes per level
resolve the last nested descendant in DOM order, recursing to the deepest leaf, no status-priority
keep that resolution live via a MutationObserver on the nested subtree
paint the resolution into the EXISTING trailing cell only while collapsed and only if not consumer-owned
add zero new CSS geometry — each nesting level paints its own independent, indented rail
add zero new ARIA machinery — rely on native <details> collapse + role=list/listitem nesting
extend the descriptor's slots only — no new props, no new events
keep the token surface, barrels, and layering byte-unchanged apart from the one new cross-reference
gate the build on an independent reviewer GO before commit
leave size non-cascading — each nesting level re-authors its own, no inheritance mechanism

edges.why: the disclosure's adoption order (n3) needs the nested slot (n2) to exist first
recursion depth (n4) composes on top of the single-level nested-slot + disclosure mechanism (n2/n3)
last-descendant resolution (n10) walks the nested slot's content, which must exist first
the observer (n11) re-runs the SAME resolution function n10 defines
the paint gate (n12) consumes the observer's recomputed value
the paint gate reads the disclosure's own open state, which n3 composes
the descriptor update (n16) documents the slot n2 adds
review (n18) grades the finished mechanism, gated on the disclosure composition landing first
review (n18) grades the collapsed-summary paint-gate correctness, which must be built first
the size-cascade negative control (n19) needs the nested slot (n2) to exist first
review (n18) grades the size-non-cascade claim too, which must be built and tested first
